question: How has the number of deaths changed from early wars to modern wars?
answer: {"answer":"Deaths do not rise steadily from early to modern wars. Early wars were mostly in the tens of thousands, spiking to 750,000 in the Civil War; deaths then reached another major peak in World War II at 405,399, but later modern conflicts dropped sharply, down to 54,246 in Korea, 58,209 in Vietnam, 294 in the Gulf War, and 6,717 in Iraq/Afghanistan.","reasoning":"I identified a horizontal bar chart showing total military deaths by war. The x-axis is labeled Deaths with ticks from 0K to 700K, using a linear scale starting at zero. The chart has one series encoded by bar length and numeric labels at the bar ends. Later wars include WWI at 116,516 and WWII at 405,399, followed by substantially lower totals in Korea, Vietnam, Gulf War, and Iraq/Afghanistan. Therefore the pattern is uneven: large peaks in the Civil War and WWII, then a marked decline in more recent conflicts.","confidence":0.95,"evidence":"American Revolutionary War: 25,000 deaths; War of 1812: 15,000; Mexican-American War: 13,283; American Civil War: 750,000; WWI: 116,516; WWII: 405,399; Korean War: 54,246; Vietnam War: 58,209; Gulf War: 294; Iraq War/Afghanistan Wars: 6,717.","interactions_performed":["Opened the chart URL with Playwright MCP","Waited for the chart to render","Inspected the page snapshot and SVG text/mark structure","Hovered representative bars including early, peak, and modern wars","Verified the active metric was Deaths"],"pitfalls_checked":["Y-axis/category axis not numeric; x-axis starts at zero","No dual y-axes present","Bars are not stacked or grouped; single-series horizontal bars","No log scale indicated; ticks are evenly spaced in 100K intervals","No linked filter appeared active","Tooltip did not add values beyond visible labels; bar-end labels were used"],"self_check":"The chart shows no simple early-to-modern increase: deaths peak at 750,000 in the Civil War and 405,399 in WWII, while the latest listed wars are far lower, especially Gulf War at 294 and Iraq/Afghanistan at 6,717."}
